title: biscutella coronopifolia subsp. ambigua
intro: Biscutella coronopifolia subsp. ambigua is a plant species in the family Brassicaceae, commonly known as the Cornish currant or small balsam-purpurin. It is a perennial herb with small, yellow flowers and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system, although more research is needed to confirm these claims. Biscutella coronopifolia subsp. ambigua is typically prepared as a herbal tea or tincture, and it can also be used in ointments or creams for topical application.
